title: GRAFT-Net: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Network for Textual and Knowledge Base Question Answering
notes: Graph expansion and graph-based evidence composition.

title: PullNet: Open Domain Question Answering with Iterative Retrieval on Knowledge Bases and Text
notes: Iteratively expands a question-specific graph.

title: Multi-hop Reading Comprehension through Question Decomposition and Rescoring
notes: Decomposes compositional questions into simpler subquestions and globally rescores decompositions.

title: Dynamically Fused Graph Network for Multi-hop Reasoning
notes: Builds and traverses an entity graph over scattered text evidence.

title: Learning to Retrieve Reasoning Paths over Wikipedia Graph for Question Answering
notes: Sequentially retrieves evidence paragraphs conditioned on earlier hops.

title: Unsupervised Question Decomposition for Question Answering
notes: Learns one-to-many decomposition without manually labeled decompositions and recomposes subanswers.

title: Scalable Multi-Hop Relational Reasoning for Knowledge-Aware Question Answering
notes: Performs multi-hop relational reasoning over extracted knowledge-graph subgraphs.

title: Hierarchical Graph Network for Multi-hop Question Answering
notes: Represents question paragraphs sentences and entities at multiple graph granularities.

title: Leveraging Passage Retrieval with Generative Models for Open Domain Question Answering
notes: Fusion-in-decoder reader over retrieved passages.

title: Learning Dense Retrieval for Multi-hop Question Answering
notes: Dense retriever trained for multi-hop evidence acquisition.

title: Baleen: Robust Multi-Hop Reasoning at Scale via Condensed Retrieval
notes: Condenses retrieved evidence between hops to control search and context growth.

title: Decomposing Complex Questions Makes Multi-Hop QA Easier and More Interpretable
notes: Uses relation extraction reader and comparison stages to form a decomposed evidence path.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
notes: Interleaves natural-language reasoning traces with environment actions such as Wikipedia lookup.

title: Interleaving Retrieval with Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Knowledge-Intensive Multi-Step Questions
notes: Interleaves generated reasoning with retrieval.

title: Active Retrieval Augmented Generation
notes: Retrieval is triggered by generation uncertainty; verify the precise multi-hop scope before using it for a focused comparison.

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve Generate and Critique through Self-Reflection
notes: Joint retrieval generation and critique policy.

title: RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval
notes: Changes retrieval granularity and context organization.

title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
notes: Evaluates retrieved context and may trigger web search plus decomposition and recomposition.

title: Adaptive-RAG: Learning to Adapt Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models through Question Complexity
notes: Selects no-retrieval single-step or iterative retrieval strategies from predicted query complexity.

title: End-to-End Beam Retrieval for Multi-Hop Question Answering
notes: Maintains multiple partial evidence hypotheses to reduce early-hop search errors.

title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
notes: Builds an entity graph and community summaries to expose local and global corpus evidence.

title: HippoRAG: Neurobiologically Inspired Long-Term Memory for Large Language Models
notes: Uses a graph-based memory index and personalized PageRank for efficient multi-hop retrieval.

title: EfficientRAG: Efficient Retriever for Multi-Hop Question Answering
notes: Decomposes a multi-hop question while pruning irrelevant subquestions and documents during retrieval.
